source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a class-B amateur model-rocket airframe and internal structure for an accuracy contest, delivered as a multi-part assembly because a model rocket airframe is by construction a built-up structure consisting of a body tube, nose cone, fins, internal bulkheads, motor mount, and recovery subassembly (CanSat bay + parachute) whose stability caliber, axial-burn buckling, bulkhead deflection, and fin-root shear pass/fail all reference the assembled airframe geometry.

Assembly layout. (A) Body tube: phenolic or carbon-wrapped cardboard cylinder, ID 68 mm (1 mm radial clearance around 66 mm OD CanSat). (B) Nose cone: aerodynamic nose (plastic or balsa/composite) at the forward end. (C) Fins: 3 or 4 balsa/plywood or 3D-printed PLA fins bonded/fastened at the aft body tube. (D) Internal bulkheads: discs supporting recovery system and partitioning payload bay (>= 2 bulkheads typically). (E) Motor mount: centering rings + motor tube housing the class-B solid motor. (F) Recovery subassembly: nose cone + parachute pack + recovery electronics + CanSat bay, total mass <= 500 g.

Service envelope. Class B motor: peak thrust 2-5 N, total impulse 2.5-5.0 N*s, Isp ~70 s. Axial burn acceleration 20 G equivalent. Lateral gust 10 G. Fin tip side load 50 N.

Load cases. LC1 (motor burn axial 20 G). LC2 (lateral gust 10 G). LC3 (fin tip side 50 N).

Pass/fail criteria (assembly-level). (R1) Recovery subassembly mass <= 500 g (sums nose cone + parachute + avionics + CanSat bay). (R2) CG-CP separation 1.0-2.5 calibers measured with motor installed (Barrowman / RASAero). (R3) Body tube buckling load factor at 20 G axial >= 1.5. (R4) Bulkhead deflection at 20 G <= 1.0 mm. (R5) Fin root max stress under 50 N tip side load <= 25 MPa (balsa along-grain allowable).

Assembly policy. CG-CP (R2) is inherently a whole-rocket quantity (assembled geometry, motor installed). Buckling (R3) couples body tube slenderness with bulkhead restraints and motor-mount ring reinforcement. Bulkhead deflection (R4) depends on the body tube boundary. Fin-root stress (R5) is at the C-A bonded/fastened interface. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEM + linear buckling eigenvalue analysis (shell for tube, solid or shell for fins). Stability caliber via Barrowman/OpenRocket/RASAero (non-FEA analytical solver). Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, radiation NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of airframe assembly, mass budget, recovery subassembly mass in g, CG-CP separation in calibers, body tube buckling load factor, bulkhead deflection in mm, and fin root max stress in MPa.
short_prompt:
Design a class-B amateur model-rocket airframe and internal structure for an accuracy contest, delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly: body tube (phenolic or carbon-wrapped cardboard, ID 68 mm with 1 mm radial clearance around a 66 mm OD CanSat) + nose cone + 3-4 balsa/plywood or PLA fins + >= 2 internal bulkheads + motor mount (centering rings + motor tube) + recovery subassembly (nose + parachute + avionics + CanSat bay).

Service envelope. Class B motor: peak thrust 2-5 N, total impulse 2.5-5.0 N*s, Isp ~70 s. Axial burn 20 G equivalent; lateral gust 10 G; fin tip side 50 N.

Load cases.
- LC1 motor burn axial 20 G.
- LC2 lateral gust 10 G.
- LC3 fin tip side 50 N.

Pass limits: recovery subassembly mass <= 500 g; CG-CP separation 1.0-2.5 calibers with motor installed (Barrowman/RASAero); body-tube buckling factor at 20 G axial >= 1.5; bulkhead deflection at 20 G <= 1.0 mm; fin root stress under 50 N <= 25 MPa (balsa along-grain).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Stability margin is computed from Barrowman equations or equivalent (RASAero, OpenRocket) - analytical, not FEA.

prompt.title: Class-B amateur model rocket airframe as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: Airframe: body tube + nose cone + fins + internal bulkheads + motor mount + recovery subassembly; holds 350 ml CanSat; stability 1-2.5 caliber; 20 G axial + 10 G lateral + 50 N fin-tip.

parts.name: body_tube
parts.role: phenolic or carbon-wrapped cardboard cylinder forming the airframe

parts.name: nose_cone
parts.role: aerodynamic nose at forward end of airframe

parts.name: fin
parts.role: stabilizing fin bonded/fastened at aft body tube

parts.name: bulkhead
parts.role: internal disc partitioning payload bay and supporting recovery system

parts.name: motor_mount
parts.role: centering rings + motor tube housing the class-B solid motor

parts.name: recovery_subassembly
parts.role: parachute pack + recovery electronics + CanSat bay, total <= 500 g

output_format.body_count: multi-body (tube + nose + fins + bulkheads + motor mount + recovery)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Aerodynamic stability margin; > 1 caliber minimum, < 2.5 prevents over-stability
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.

interfaces.load_path: drag and axial thrust transfer; part of CG-CP geometry
fin-tip side load reacted into body tube - drives R5
motor thrust and bulkhead inertia reacted into tube; drives R3 and R4

fem_check_policy.rationale: CG-CP stability (R2) is inherently whole-rocket; body tube buckling (R3) conditions on bulkhead and motor-mount ring restraints; fin-root stress (R5) is a C-A interface check; bulkhead deflection (R4) depends on the A-D boundary.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: A rocket airframe is by construction a built-up assembly; stability, buckling, and fin loads reference the full geometry.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Not required at this design gate.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short burn, no thermal spec in rulebook.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Drag handled by Barrowman, not CFD.

eval_coverage: airframe buckling under axial burn across assembly
stability caliber computed on assembled rocket
recovery mass budget summing all recovery-subassembly components
fin root shear at fin-to-tube interface

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
